floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: the p_b4/p_b6 aliases are plausibly INERT -- the feared store-to-reload CSE fold has to survive two intervening variable-address stores (sh 0x7DE/0x7DC($s2)) and GCC 2.7.2 cse.c inv

frontier.hypothesis: Direct re-reads `(s16)D_800A32B4` / `(s16)D_800A32B6` (aliases deleted) byte-match: the intervening variable-address stores at :13252-13253 already invalidate the store-to-reload equivalence, so the lh reloads at s:64/s:113 emerge naturally.
frontier.mechanism: cse.c note_mem_written/invalidate_memory conservatism on stores through non-constant addresses + combine's sign_extend(mem:HI) -> lh narrowing for single-use loads.
frontier.next_probe: Apply the two-line replacement + delete :13234-13235, `sandbox func_80060768 --disable all` (expect 0), verify-oracle, queue done.

frontier.hypothesis: If the fold happens anyway, an ordering/structure lever short of any alias may still break the equivalence -- e.g. spelling the clamp read before the 0x7DC store is NOT viable (target byte order fixes sh 0x7DC before lh), so the realistic fallback is the annotated pointer-alias route pending the ruling below.
frontier.mechanism: cse.c equivalence lifetime between `sh gp_rel(sym)` and the re-read; the only C-visible events in that window are the two arg0-based stores.
frontier.next_probe: Read the failing sandbox diff to confirm the fold shape (sll/sra of the increment register at s:64), capture the cse dump (-dc) naming the surviving equivalence, then surface the ruling_question with that evidence attached.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Two single-use pointer aliases (p_b4, p_b6) fall explicitly outside the pointer-rmw-global-sanctioned carve-out — that rule requires at least one load AND one store through the pointer (an actual RMW sequence); here the RMW on D_800A32B4/D_800A32B6 uses direct access and the pointers each have exactly one read use. Worker must: (a) replace `(s16)*p_b4` and `(s16)*p_b6` with `(s16)D_800A32B4` / `(s16)D_800A32B6` (or the appropriate direct signed cast) and confirm the oracle still holds — if it does the pointers were redundant and the clean form is the fix; (b) if oracle breaks, find a pure-C form that produces the signed halfword `lh` load without a pointer alias (e.g. check whether `(s16)D_800A32B4` already produces `lh` via GCC's sign-extend inference on a cast, or restructure the comparison).  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
